title: Hadoop MapReduce Architecture
description:
Hadoop MapReduce is a software framework for easily writing applications which process vast amounts of data (multi-terabyte data-sets) parallelly on large clusters (thousands of nodes).

The system is composed of:
- A distributed file system (HDFS) that provides high-throughput access to application data.
- Map tasks that process data and write intermediate, sorted data to the distributed file system.
- A shuffle and sort phase that merges the intermediate data and prepares it for the reduce tasks.
- Reduce tasks that process the intermediate data and write the final output.

The system is scalable, fault-tolerant, and self-healing. It provides high utilization of computing resources and low cost of operation.

Technologies used:
- Java
- HDFS
- YARN (resource manager, node manager)

TITLE: Hadoop MapReduce Architecture

DESCRIPTION:
Hadoop MapReduce is a software framework for easily writing applications which process vast amounts of data (multi-terabyte data-sets) parallelly on large clusters (thousands of nodes).

The system is composed of:
- A distributed file system (HDFS) that provides high-throughput access to application data.
- Map tasks that process data and write intermediate, sorted data to the distributed file system.
- A shuffle and sort phase that merges the intermediate data and prepares it for the reduce tasks.
- Reduce tasks that process the intermediate data and write the final output.

The system is scalable, fault-tolerant, and self-healing. It provides high utilization of computing resources and low cost of operation.

Technologies used:
- Java
- HDFS
- YARN (resource manager, node manager)
